title: capparis spinosa subsp. cartilaginea
intro: Capparis spinosa subsp. cartilaginea, also known as the cartilaginous caper or Cretan caper, is a subspecies of the caper plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Capparis spinosa subsp. cartilaginea include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
